frameworks.name: slot model
frameworks.one_line: visual working memory holds a fixed small number of discrete item slots

frameworks.name: flexible resource model
frameworks.one_line: a continuous memory resource is shared across all items

frameworks.name: slot-plus-resource hybrid model
frameworks.one_line: discrete slots each hold a divisible resource that sets per-item precision

frameworks.name: cognitive neuroscience of working memory load
frameworks.one_line: physiological load indices used to adjudicate capacity allocation accounts

core_question: How is visual working memory capacity allocated across items — via discrete slots, a flexible resource, or a hybrid?
key_assumptions: capacity for visual working memory is limited
allocation patterns can be inferred from behavior plus physiological load indices
competing models make dissociable predictions about per-item precision
key_findings: evidence is reviewed in favor of both slot and flexible resource accounts
a hybrid slot-plus-resource model better fits the existing literature
cognitive-neuroscience load measures help adjudicate between accounts
interpretations: neither pure slot nor pure resource models suffice; a hybrid account is preferred
physiological load indices give more direct access to allocation patterns than behavior alone
